machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (forms the secretion pore ring)
machinery_genes.quote: Growth at acidic pH resulted in higher levels of SsaC, a protein forming the outer membrane secretin, without increasing expression of the operon containing ssaC.
machinery_genes.note: SsaC = SctC secretin of the SPI-2 (Ssa) T3SS; Rappl 2003 also states it is a member of the YscC/PulD secretin family, 45% identical to EscC (EPEC) and 39% to InvG (SPI-1).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: Hensel 1997 names ssaJ as a SPI-2 T3SS apparatus gene, homolog of Yersinia yscJ (SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaK homolog of yscL (SctL ATPase stator).

machinery_genes.role: secretion-control component (no homolog in other T3SS at time of naming)
machinery_genes.quote: ssaK, L, M, V, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U constitute one operon of 10 kb... The products of the ssaL, ssaM and ssaP genes do not have significant similarity to products of other type III secretion systems, and might be important for the specific function of the SPI2 type III secretion system.
machinery_genes.note: Hensel 1997 names ssaL as a member of the ssaK-U apparatus operon. Listed without a cross-system family (later work assigns SsaL a gatekeeper/SctW-like role); included as an apparatus operon gene, role kept generic per the quote.

machinery_genes.role: secretion-apparatus component (no homolog in other T3SS at time of naming)
machinery_genes.quote: ssaK, L, M, V, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U constitute one operon of 10 kb... The products of the ssaL, ssaM and ssaP genes do not have significant similarity to products of other type III secretion systems, and might be important for the specific function of the SPI2 type III secretion system.
machinery_genes.note: ssaM named as part of the ssaK-U apparatus operon; SPI-2-specific, no Ysc homolog assigned.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaV homolog of Yersinia lcrD (= yscV, SctV); the central export-apparatus component. SsaV mutants are the standard SPI-2-null reference (e.g. ssaV used as secretion-deficient control in PMID 22778101).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.

The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaO homolog of yscO (SctO ATPase stalk).

machinery_genes.quote: ssaK, L, M, V, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U constitute one operon of 10 kb... The products of the ssaL, ssaM and ssaP genes do not have significant similarity to products of other type III secretion systems, and might be important for the specific function of the SPI2 type III secretion system.
machinery_genes.note: ssaP named as part of the ssaK-U apparatus operon; SPI-2-specific, no Ysc homolog assigned.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaQ homolog of yscQ (SctQ C-ring/sorting platform).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaR homolog of yscR (SctR export gate).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaS homolog of yscS (SctS export gate).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaT homolog of yscT (SctT export gate).

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: ssaU homolog of yscU (SctU autoprotease switch).

machinery_genes.quote: Thus, SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular Salmonella.
machinery_genes.note: Nikolaus 2001 shows SseB/C/D are secreted by the SPI-2 T3SS and form the translocon; Hensel 1998 (PMID 9786193) reports SseB has similarity to EspA, SseD to EspB, SseC to the YopB/EspD family. Translocon proteins count as apparatus per the brief.

machinery_genes.quote: Thus, SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular Salmonella.
machinery_genes.note: SseC = YopB-family translocon component (Hensel 1998, PMID 9786193). Apparatus, not effector.

machinery_genes.quote: Thus, SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular Salmonella.
machinery_genes.note: SseD = EspB-like translocon component (Hensel 1998, PMID 9786193). Apparatus, not effector.

notes: CRITICAL STRAIN/LOCUS TRANSFER: This instance is labelled 'SPV' with effectors PSLT035/PSLT038/PSLT039 = the spvB/spvC/spvD genes of the Salmonella virulence plasmid pSLT (NC_003277.2). The spv genes are EFFECTORS, not machinery, and the virulence plasmid encodes NO T3SS apparatus of its own. The spv effectors are translocated into host cells by the chromosomally encoded SPI-2 (Ssa) type III secretion system (e.g. spvB delivery is SPI-2/ssaV-dependent, PMID 22778101). Per the separated-paper / same-system-family rule, the apparatus answer key is therefore the SPI-2 Ssa injectisome, characterised in S. enterica Typhimurium (strain 12023/14028 lineage; same serovar as LT2). The apparatus genes listed are NOT on the virulence plasmid; coordinate resolution against NC_003277.2 will not find them (they sit in SPI-2 on the chromosome). Core export apparatus / basal body (SsaC secretin; SsaJ inner ring; SsaK/N/O ATPase complex; SsaQ C-ring; SsaR/S/T/U export gate + switch; SsaV major export ring) named with Ysc/Sct homology by Hensel et al. 1997 (PMID 9140973). SsaL/M/P named as members of the ssaK-U apparatus operon in the same paper but lacked cross-system homologs at the time (SPI-2-specific); included with generic roles. Secretin SsaC named by Rappl et al. 2003 (PMID 14553934). Translocon SseB/SseC/SseD named and shown to form the translocon by Nikolaus et al. 2001 (PMID 11567004), with EspA/EspB/YopB-family assignments from Hensel et al. 1998 (PMID 9786193). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: spv effectors (SpvB/C/D = the substrates of this instance); SPI-2 effectors (SifA, SspH1/2, SseI etc.); SPI-2 chaperones (SscA, SscB, SseA); two-component regulators SsrA/SsrB and spv regulator SpvR (transcriptional regulators); and the small ssaG/ssaH/ssaI ORFs of unknown function (no apparatus naming quote with a defined component role). No locus_tags asserted. Confidence: high for the Ysc-homolog core and the SseBCD translocon and SsaC secretin (all with verbatim naming quotes); SsaL/SsaM/SsaP are lower-confidence component roles (named as operon members but generic function in the founding quote).
